title: No linux binary in the asset list
body:
/kind bug

Hey, i'm just wondering why there is no more linux built published? Since v4 release the [podman-remote-static.tar.gz](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v4.0.3/podman-remote-static.tar.gz) asset contains a windows executable:

abc@def:~/Downloads$ file podman-remote-static
podman-remote-static: PE32+ executable (console) x86-64 (stripped to external PDB), for MS Windows

I assume this is a bug because this was fine in v3 releases.

comments.body: I hope podman itself released in static binary for linux, as the way that consul, traefik etc. other go apps did.

> I hope podman itself released in static binary for linux, as the way that consul, traefik etc. other go apps did.

Only the remote client. The native `podman` binary would be much too restricted to reasonably support it.
I'm building on Linux, using the Makefile's `podman-remote-static` target. If that's not producing a Linux binary anymore that sounds like a pretty significant bug in the Makefile?
I see podman-remote-static in the asset list.  Since we don't plan on shipping Podman that way, I am going to close this issue.

Isn’t the complaint that the Podman-remote-static binary isn’t a valid
Linux executable and is instead being built as a windows binary?

On Thu, Apr 14, 2022 at 10:14 Daniel J Walsh ***@***.***>
wrote:

> Closed #13833 <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13833>.
>

> ***@***.***>

How does that binary get there?  Is this done by computer script or human?

Human, I build it via the ‘make podman-remote-static’ target

On Thu, Apr 14, 2022 at 10:52 Daniel J Walsh ***@***.***>
wrote:

> Reopened #13833 <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13833>.
>
> —
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13833#event-6435196130>, or
> unsubscribe

> .
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.Message ID:
> ***@***.***>
>
When I build with that command in makefile, it works for me and the executable is correct.

Hi,

In the end, will something be done about the fact that release 4.0.3 "[podman-remote-static.tar.gz](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v4.0.3/podman-remote-static.tar.gz)" deliverable contains a windows executable ? (Note: 4.0.2 had a Linux executable). Will the asset be re-uploaded with the Linux binary ?

Cheers,
Romain
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I'm going to close this. We've fixed the release scripts and makefile to generate correct binaries, so new releases are fixed.
